blocks.content: Kit got the window open one inch at a time, the old wood grinding in the frame, teeth clenched against every sound. The latch was corroded but it turned. The sash went up. The summer air came in — and it had never felt so good, the smell of the yard, the grass, the ordinary world.

He helped Eli through first. Eli swung out, hung from the sill by his hands, and dropped. A soft thud in the tall grass. He rolled up immediately, unhurt, and looked back up. Savannah next — more careful, quieter, she barely made a sound when she landed and had her feet under her in a second.
The footsteps were on the upper landing now. The hallway door was right there.

She looked at him — one full second, the kind that has a lot in it — and went to the window. He helped her over the sill, her hands last on the frame, and for a moment they were eye to eye through it: her on the outside hanging on, him on the inside letting go.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Kit. Promise me you make it out.
Not a request. Not a question. The kind of thing you say when you need to hear the words come back.
She dropped. He heard her land. He spun back toward the window and reached for the sill—
